title: Episode 24 Rei in Terminal Dogma QuantumRei
display_title: Episode 24 Rei in Terminal Dogma QuantumRei
wikitext:
[]: Episode 24: Rei in Terminal Dogma/Quantum!Rei             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 11, 2014 3:27 am Alright, so in Episode 24, Kaworu and Shinji descend to Terminal Dogma, Kaworu discovers Lilith we all know what happens. While they're down there, a new AT-Field appears, the strongest ever recorded, and blocks all sensors and communications down there; we then see Rei is responsible for this new AT-Field, and she observes as Unit-01 and Shinji kill Kaworu, the final Angel. Recently, I was lurking around online, and came across a conversation where somebody was explaining Quantum!Rei to somebody, and during their explanation they included the Rei seen in Terminal Dogma as part of the Quantum!Rei hypothesis. I eventually responded to the original poster; Sachi wrote: Just read your last post about Quantum!Rei, and was intrigued by your assertion that the Rei that appeared before Kaworu in Terminal Dogma in 24 was also an appearance of Quantum!Rei. How are you certain it's not Rei III? It's incredible that she made it down there on her own, but she does possess some level of control over her AT-Field now. It just seems more likely than Quantium!Rei, who never actual interacts with anything and is more of an observer, unlike the Rei we see in 24. Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 11, 2014 4:18 am Since Quantum!Rei never make more than blink appearances in the rest of the series and never was more than an observer(except maybe her role of reaping mankind's souls during Instrumentality), I doubt it was her against Kaworu. Besides that wouldn't make any sense since Rei III is here and alive and her previous scene with Kaworu when he said that they are similar was a huge foreshadowing, so it wouldn't make any sense to use her as that moment to confirm to the audience that yes, alongside being a clone Rei has angelic powers, and extremely strong ones at that.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 11, 2014 4:41 am Hmm... interesting idea. On the one hand we've always just assumed that was Rei3 because of the foreshadowing and whatnot, but it does make a certain kind of sense that Quantum!Rei would be there. If so, we don't have to wonder how the hell she got in there, and it also means that Rei3 might have never had that kind of ATF power until after absorbing Adam and rejoining Lilith. Kaworu may even have seen himself in this Rei's appearance and known that meant this was meant to happen, explaining his sudden change of heart. Also there is a reason for her to be there... she's collecting Kaworu's soul. I don't know if I'm sold on the idea completely, but it's halfway plausible at least.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 11, 2014 5:06 am She doesn't collect any other angel souls (Adam-type), so why Kaworu's? It's not a lilim soul, which are the ones Lilith would have an interest in.

[]:             Quote Post by Sephizim » Tue Feb 11, 2014 9:18 am pwhodges wrote: She doesn't collect any other angel souls (Adam-type), so why Kaworu's? It's not a lilim soul, which are the ones Lilith would have an interest in.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 11, 2014 9:30 am I don't picture the soul collection as being anything of Gendou's bidding, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Feb 11, 2014 11:14 am If it's "quantum Rei" then it's the only time the apparition is shown in close up, not shown as vaguely mirage-like, etc. The visual does not strongly suggest it.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Tue Feb 11, 2014 12:35 pm I always assumed that it really was Rei (as in Rei, herself, physically, without any Quantum mumbo-jumbo) in Terminal Dogma in that episode. I thought that the appearance of the second huge AT-Field was her very briefly using her ATF to levitate (which we know she can do, as seen in EoE) in order to reach that high rafter she was on. Notably in every confirmed Quantum!Rei case Quantum!Rei trypically stands very close to the victim when collecting the soul, and only appears at the instant of death when the victim is about to die; she doesn't hang around. The fact that Rei is very far away from Kaworu in this scene and is present for an extended period of time, rather than appearing and disappearing, suggests that it really was Rei, watching Kaworu's death. Kaworu also seems to be trying to communicate something to Rei with his 'smile of betrayal' so once again it's probably not a quantum phantom.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 11, 2014 12:36 pm There is NOTHING to suggests that (She doesn't dissapear imediately, and is later seen with Gendo during the cleanup, suggesting he picked her up/sent her. ) By that logic, we might as well suggest every shot of Rei standing somewhere on her own is actually quantum magic. If you're wondering why she didn't use that power before, note how it imediately showed up on the AT-field detectors.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Feb 11, 2014 12:38 pm Consider the Bridge Bunnies, gentlefolk! They don't just sit around dropping exposition all day for nothing. If you watch episode 24, just before Unit-01 seizes Kaworu, they reel off a string of lines. Episode 24 wrote: Hyuga: It's another AT-field. Aoba: A field as strong as the first one [Kaworu's unprecedentedly strong ATF] has appeared around Terminal Dogma. Maya: He's right, it's breaking through the other AT-field. Misato: What is it?! Another Angel?! Aoba: I don't know, I can't confirm it - God, it just disappeared! Misato: Disappeared - The angel?" [cut to Rei] [close up on Rei]

[]:             Quote Post by Eleven » Tue Feb 11, 2014 1:00 pm ElMariachi wrote: Since Quantum!Rei never make more than blink appearances in the rest of the series and never was more than an observer (except maybe her role of reaping mankind's souls during Instrumentality), I doubt it was her against Kaworu. Besides that wouldn't make any sense since Rei III is here and alive and her previous scene with Kaworu when he said that they are similar was a huge foreshadowing, so it wouldn't make any sense to use her as that moment to confirm to the audience that yes, alongside being a clone Rei has angelic powers, and extremely strong ones at that.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 11, 2014 1:06 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: Consider the Bridge Bunnies, gentlefolk! They don't just sit around dropping exposition all day for nothing. If you watch episode 24, just before Unit-01 seizes Kaworu, they reel off a string of lines. ... So I think Rei has a personal super-Anti-ATF like Kaworu has a personal ATF, and Rei is an angel/seed like Kaworu is as well. If that Gary-Stu gets to fly, I say Rei can tang people at will.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 11, 2014 1:25 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: So I think Rei has a personal super-Anti-ATF like Kaworu has a personal ATF, and Rei is an angel/seed like Kaworu is as well. If that Gary-Stu gets to fly, I say Rei can tang people at will.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 11, 2014 1:58 pm I've always operated under the assumption that it was Rei III that we saw down there in Dogma. If it was foreshadowing and Kaworu was seeing her before he died, then that would mean that Quantum Rei were reaching back into that particular point in time, with would be an unbelievable mindfuck. Hrm. Now that I think deeply about it, I'm split on the issue.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Feb 11, 2014 2:10 pm What if it was Quantum Rei? An interesting idea. It would explain why Rei's AT Field was uh....stronger than Kaworu's, for some reason? It's kind of elegant, though I still prefer Rei 3. Rei doesn't collect angelic/Adam souls, bluh. Hahaha says who? Besides, aside from maybe Rei's and Kaworu's, all souls are effectively the same once you shed the meat sacks. That's why they can be recycled in terraforming Seed events.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Feb 11, 2014 2:46 pm Just so we clear here, my contribution to the discussion is intended to answer OP. I'm just saying the Bridge Bunny dialogue presents "talk about thing like Kaworu - cut to Rei", suggesting that Rei (a thing like Kaworu, unlike a G.A.) is the one in Terminal Dogma. Sachi wrote: What about those lines suggest that Rei has an Anti-ATF as opposed to a regular one? (snip)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 11, 2014 2:55 pm AuraTwilight wrote: What if it was Quantum Rei? An interesting idea. It would explain why Rei's AT Field was uh....stronger than Kaworu's, for some reason? .

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 11, 2014 4:46 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Rei doesn't collect angelic/Adam souls, bluh. Hahaha says who? Besides, aside from maybe Rei's and Kaworu's, all souls are effectively the same once you shed the meat sacks. That's why they can be recycled in terraforming Seed events.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Feb 11, 2014 4:49 pm pwhodges wrote: Where do the fruits of knowledge and of life actually reside? Are they associated with the souls?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 11, 2014 5:43 pm That account of the fruits would seem far too simple - why would the FAR (in NGE, I know we don't know about NTE) take such trouble to try to keep them apart (which went disastrously wrong on Earth) if they can just be made anyway?
